items.title: Dashboard: static index shell, server-backed state, no periodic HTML rewrite
items.body: The dashboard MUST NOT depend on the console app rewriting index.html to pick up DB changes. The browser loads current work graph state from the web server (Kestrel or equivalent). index.html is a stable shell: it is not regenerated on a timer; client-side code fetches snapshot (or equivalent) and refreshes rendered content every 180 seconds. Zero requirement to re-emit HTML for routine updates.

items.title: Audit: emit, watch, serve vs target behavior
items.body: Document current behavior: dashboard watch rewrites index.html; emit embeds snapshot; serve already fetches /api/snapshot. List gaps vs requirement (no file rewrite for data cadence, 180s poll).

items.title: Immutable dashboard shell (index.html not regenerated for data updates)
items.body: Treat index.html (and inline assets) as a static template: ship once or version rarely. No scheduled or watch-driven rewrite of the HTML file for DB changes. Optional one-time materialize on first serve if file missing.

items.title: Server-only path for live graph JSON (no embedded snapshot for updates)
items.body: All routine updates flow through HTTP JSON (e.g. /api/snapshot, optional /api/item). Kestrel serves shell + API; SQLite read on each request or cached with explicit invalidation policy documented.

items.title: Client: load and refresh board every 180 seconds (no meta refresh, no file reload)
items.body: Vanilla JS (or existing pattern): initial fetch on load; setInterval 180s to refetch snapshot and re-render columns/detail without full page navigation. Remove reliance on meta http-equiv refresh and on watch rewriting disk for live mode. Default interval 180s; optional CLI flag to override.

items.title: CLI UX: serve as primary live workflow; narrow watch/emit
items.body: Position dashboard serve (or rename) as the default live experience. dashboard watch that re-emit HTML on interval is deprecated or gated for offline snapshot export only. README + skill: when to use serve vs emit.

items.title: Tests and documentation for no-rewrite dynamic dashboard
items.body: Tests: serve path does not rewrite index on poll; client timer 180s contract. Docs: security (loopback), caching headers for static shell if applicable.

items.title: Write gap matrix: file rewrite vs in-browser refresh vs serve
Spec: when index.html may be written (first install only) vs forbidden paths
Eliminate or gate watch loop that rewrites index.html for live viewing
Freeze API contract for dynamic loads; document cache semantics
Implement 180s client poll and full in-DOM rerender from JSON
Ensure served/dynamic mode has no meta refresh; no dependency on disk file change
CLI: default --refresh-seconds to 180 for served dynamic mode; help text
Automated test: N polls do not mutate index.html mtime/content hash
README + SKILL: dynamic serve-first workflow and 180s behavior
